feedback.text: There is an urgent need to regulate the video game industry. Unfortunately, publishers and app shop operators, including console shops, all exploit the fact that small consumers, i.e. us, are not protected by law. As large organisations, they create a monopoly, the only way to buy games from digital stores, with prices being regulated by them. And by taking advantage of this loophole, video game publishers also like to pull people down and eliminate the existence of their products. Video games are artistic products. It works by writers, graphics, animators, actors, directors, musicians, and I could still be listed. Publishers, on the other hand, treat these products as they no longer generate money for them as they do with waste. Can we imagine that a film will be destroyed forever, erased from the world only because it is no longer bought? Unfortunately, this is what happens in the video game industry. We need help to ensure that our favourite games, to which we feel emotionally, do not disappear because of the capitalist interests of a few financial managers. It is a lie that it is not possible to save games. Yes, the games of the 1990s can still be played 100 % (if the publisher does not try to prevent them from running again for selfless reasons, for example by banning emulators), their multiplayer part is still operated by the community. In the 2010s, micro-transactions, loot boxes and gambling were deliberately transformed into video games to stop operating if they did not bring money. This was indeed a self-sustainable business decision at the beginning of the 2010s and never arose before it, so that it would not be possible to play a game after the servers were shut down, if not with a LAN connection. Internet connection is not required because it is absolutely necessary to run the game, LAN could be sufficient. The net requirement is to make the most of people, to eviscerate the pay community and, if necessary, to kill the game, so that people are forced to turn to a new part or a new game, thus trying another money. We need help to ensure that what we bought once, which is not cheap at any time, but they don’t buy it from us with the tricks that we have contracted. This must be illegal and theft is theft. Thank you for helping you save this part of the culture, as publishers have already gone away, destroying the players’ experience and the many years of work of the developers. They should not be influenced by publishers, regrettably they have a commercial interest in being afraid of technical constraints. If they wanted, they could change the practice, a number of developers have demonstrated this in the past, and this was not a question before 2010.
